patches.hypothesis: The refined patch handles the END_OBJECT token through a more robust approach and includes proper edge case handling for empty JSON objects.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug was caused by the method not handling the case where the current token of the parser is END_OBJECT, which is the case when the JSON object is empty. The method was trying to read the next token even if it was END_OBJECT, which caused the exception.

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the method not handling the case when the current token is END_OBJECT. The method should check for this case and return an empty ObjectNode instead of throwing an exception.
The deserialize method was not handling the case where the current token is END_OBJECT, resulting in an error when trying to deserialize an empty ObjectNode.

patches.hypothesis: The issue is caused by the method not handling the case where the current token is END_OBJECT, it only handles the case where it is START_OBJECT and FIELD_NAME. The solution is to add a check for END_OBJECT and handle it accordingly.
The issue is caused by the method not being able to handle the case where the current token is END_OBJECT, which is the first token of an empty object.
patches.changes: The fix involves adding a new condition to check if the current token is END_OBJECT and returning an empty ObjectNode in that case.
